info.title: MecaPy API
info.description: API for MecaPy with Keycloak authentication and database persistence

get.description:
Return the detail of a FunctionVersion (FRO-fn-04).

Visibility follows the parent package's rules (FRO-pkg-08/10):
- public packages: any user (authenticated or not).
- internal: members of the package's organization.
- private: owner only.

List recent jobs submitted by the authenticated user.

Jobs whose package is no longer visible to the caller (FRO-pkg-10) are filtered out.

Get status of a running or completed job.

Enforces FRO-pkg-10: 403 if the user no longer has access to the job's package.
Get the result of a completed job (with FRO-pkg-10 enforcement).

Stream execution logs for a job (:spec:`FRO-jobs-08` v2).

Returns only chunks emitted since index ``since`` so the frontend can
poll incrementally while the job runs. ``done=True`` once the job has
reached a terminal status — callers should stop polling then.

Enforces FRO-pkg-10: 403 if the caller lost access to the job's package.

Get information about the currently authenticated user.

This endpoint requires authentication.
Example protected route that requires authentication.
Example admin route that requires the admin role.

List packages with permission-aware filtering (FRO-pkg-08/09/13).

Visible set:
- ``public`` packages → everyone, even anonymous callers (FRO-pkg-09).
- ``internal`` packages → members of the package's organization.
- ``private`` packages → the owner only.

Archive filter (FRO-pkg-05): ``include_archived`` lets the caller see
their own archived packages.

Yank filter (FRO-pkg-13): yanked packages are hidden from
discoverability (listing) by default for all callers. The owner can
pass ``include_yanked=true`` to see their own yanked packages.
Workflows tiers that already cite a yanked package continue to
resolve the FunctionVersion via the direct route — yank only affects
the listing, not the storage.

parameters.name: include_yanked

schema.description: Include own yanked packages (FRO-pkg-13). Yanked packages are hidden from public/internal listings by default ; this flag lets the owner see their own yanked entries.
schema.title: Include Yanked

parameters.description: Include own yanked packages (FRO-pkg-13). Yanked packages are hidden from public/internal listings by default ; this flag lets the owner see their own yanked entries.

get.description:
Get details of a specific package.

Public packages are accessible without authentication.
Private packages require authentication and ownership.

Parameters
----------
package_id : str
    Unique identifier of the package

Returns
-------
PackageResponse
    Complete package details with functions

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found
    - 403: Access denied (private package, not owner)

patch.description:
Update the visibility of a package.

Only the package owner can change visibility. The manifest `visibility`
field sets the initial value at deploy time; this endpoint allows
post-deployment overrides without requiring a redeploy.

Parameters
----------
package_id : str
    Unique identifier of the package
body : PackageVisibilityUpdate
    New visibility setting

Returns
-------
PackageResponse
    Updated package details

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found
    - 403: Not the package owner

post.description:
Archive a package (reversible). Archived packages are hidden from non-owners.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found
    - 403: Not the package owner
    - 409: Package is already archived

Unarchive a previously archived package.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found
    - 403: Not the package owner
    - 409: Package is not archived

Yank a package (FRO-pkg-13).

Distinct de l'archive (FRO-pkg-05) : retire le package de la
discoverability publique (listings, marketplace) tout en préservant
les références descendantes — workflows tiers continuent à pouvoir
l'exécuter. Pattern registry (npm/Cargo yank).

Idempotent : yank d'un package déjà yanké est un no-op (preserve
le yanked_at original).

Pas applicable aux packages ``private`` (pas de listing public à
retirer) → 400.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found
    - 403: Not the package owner
    - 400: visibility=private (yank not applicable)

Un-yank a previously yanked package (FRO-pkg-13).

Idempotent : unyank d'un package non-yanké est un no-op.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found
    - 403: Not the package owner

Upload a File input ahead of execution (FRO-runtime-02).

The file is stored under
``s3://{CLOUD_BUCKET_UPLOADS}/uploads/{user_id}/{upload_id}/{var}.{ext}``
and indexed in Redis (24h TTL). The returned ``upload_id`` can be
referenced by ``POST /packages/{id}/functions/{name}/execute`` via
its ``file_inputs`` map.

Validation:
- Var name must reference a typed File input port on the function.
- File extension must be accepted by the port's ``File[ext1,ext2,…]``
  constraint (any extension is allowed when the port is plain ``File``).

Returns
-------
FileUploadResponse
    Metadata describing the stored upload.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: package or function not found.
    - 403: caller cannot execute this package.
    - 422: var_name is not a File-typed input, or extension rejected.
    - 500: S3 storage failure.

Execute a function from a deployed package asynchronously.

Submits an execution job to the worker queue via Redis.
The function runs in an isolated Docker container; results are
available via GET /jobs/{job_id}/result once completed.

Access control:
- Public package → any authenticated user can execute
- Private package → owner only

Parameters
----------
package_id : str
    Unique identifier of the package
function_name : str
    Name of the function to execute (as declared in mecapy.yml)
request : FunctionExecuteRequest
    Execution payload
current_user : UserInfo
    Authenticated user

Returns
-------
JobSubmitResponse
    Job ID for tracking execution via GET /jobs/{job_id}/result

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package or function not found
    - 403: Private package, not the owner
    - 500: Worker submission or S3 extraction failed

Deploy a multi-function package from a Git repository.

This endpoint clones a Git repository containing a mecapy.yml manifest,
validates the package version, extracts all functions, and deploys them as
serverless functions. Supports both public and private repositories.

The manifest format is simple and minimal with automatic schema introspection.
Schemas are auto-generated from Python type hints and docstrings, with optional
explicit overrides when needed.

Workflow:
1. Clone Git repository
2. Find and parse mecapy.yml manifest
2.5. Validate manifest version against Git tags
3. Validate package version (409 if exists)
4. Upload package source to S3
5. Create Package and PackageVersion records
6. Extract and deploy each function (with auto-introspection)
7. Load and validate test cases (if enabled)
8. Update package status
9. Cleanup temporary files

Security features:
- Validates Git URL against allowed providers
- Enforces SemVer versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
- Prevents version conflicts (immutable versions)
- Validates import paths and function code
- Scans for dangerous imports

Parameters
----------
request : PackageFromGitRequest
    Package deployment configuration including Git URL, ref, and deployment strategy
current_user : UserInfo
    Authenticated user (injected by dependency)
session : AsyncSession
    Database session

Returns
-------
PackageDeployResponse
    Deployment results with package info and deployment statistics

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 400: Invalid Git URL, manifest not found, or malformed manifest
    - 404: Package manifest (mecapy.yml) not found
    - 409: Package version already exists (version conflict)
    - 422: Code validation failed or invalid import paths
    - 500: Deployment or Git cloning failed

Example
-------
    ```python
    # Deploy entire package from GitHub
    response = await client.post(
        "/packages/from-git",
        json={
            "git_url": "https://github.com/mecapy/boulonnerie.git",
            "git_ref": "main",
            "deploy_strategy": "all",
        },
    )

    # Deploy specific functions from package
    response = await client.post(
        "/packages/from-git",
        json={
            "git_url": "https://github.com/mecapy/boulonnerie.git",
            "git_ref": "v1.2.0",
            "deploy_strategy": "selective",
            "functions": ["calcul-contrainte", "calcul-cisaillement"],
        },
    )
    ```

Notes
-----
- Package versions are immutable. To update, increment version in manifest.
- Function versions are automatically created and linked to package version.
- Checksums are calculated for audit and integrity verification.
- Test cases validation (if enabled) runs after deployment.

Deploy a multi-function package from a ZIP archive.

Accepts a ZIP file containing a ``mecapy.yml`` manifest and the package
source code. The archive may be flat (files at root) or wrapped in a single
top-level directory (as produced by ``git archive`` or GitHub's Download ZIP).

The deployment pipeline is identical to ``/packages/from-git`` starting from
manifest parsing — the only difference is the source acquisition step.

Parameters
----------
file : UploadFile
    ZIP archive (.zip). Maximum 50 MB.
deploy_strategy : str
    ``"all"`` to deploy every function, ``"selective"`` to deploy only the
    functions listed in the ``functions`` form field.
functions : str, optional
    Comma-separated function names (only used when ``deploy_strategy="selective"``).

Returns
-------
PackageDeployResponse
    Same structure as ``/packages/from-git``.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 400: Invalid ZIP, manifest not found, or malformed manifest
    - 409: Package version conflict
    - 413: Archive too large (> 50 MB)
    - 422: Code validation failed
    - 500: Unexpected deployment error

Update an existing package by deploying a new version from Git.

The caller must own the package. The manifest version must match the
existing package name and be strictly greater than the current version.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 404: Package not found or manifest missing
    - 403: Caller is not the package owner
    - 409: Name mismatch, version conflict, or version not incremented
    - 422: Function extraction failed
    - 500: Git clone or unexpected failure

Update an existing package by uploading a new version as a ZIP archive.

Mirrors :func:`update_package_from_git` but reads the sources from a ZIP
upload instead of a Git repository. Only the package owner may call this.

Raises
------
HTTPException
    - 400: Invalid ZIP, manifest not found, or malformed manifest
    - 403: Caller is not the package owner
    - 404: Package not found
    - 409: Name mismatch or version not incremented
    - 413: Archive too large (> 50 MB)
    - 422: Function extraction failed

get.description: List organizations the current user belongs to (FRO-org-02).

post.description: Create an organization. The creator is automatically added as Admin (FRO-org-01).

get.description: Get the detail of an organization — members + role — restricted to members (FRO-org-03).

delete.description: Delete an organization (FRO-org-05). Admin-only, slug confirmation required, no packages attached.

patch.description: Change the role of a member (FRO-org-07). Admin-only, enforces FRO-org-06.

get.description: Return the canonical type catalog grouped by category.

post.description:
Check whether a source output type can feed a target input type.

Used by the workflow editor (pre-validation) and by the workflow API
(validation at save time). Returns ALLOWED with a reason or REFUSED
with a reason.
Create a new empty workflow. Owner becomes the current user.

get.description:
List workflows accessible to the current user (or anonymous for public).

Note: visibility is computed per workflow (FRO-wkf-10) from its latest
version graph. For large lists this means one fv_to_package lookup per
workflow — acceptable at the current scale, will be batched if needed.

Archive filter (FRO-wkf-18): by default only ``active`` workflows are
listed. Pass ``include_archived=true`` to include archived workflows
owned by the current user (anonymous callers always get the active
subset only).

parameters.name: include_archived

schema.description: Include workflows that have been archived (FRO-wkf-18). Only the archived workflows owned by the current user are returned, anonymous callers never see archived items.
schema.title: Include Archived

parameters.description: Include workflows that have been archived (FRO-wkf-18). Only the archived workflows owned by the current user are returned, anonymous callers never see archived items.

get.description: Return full workflow detail (including latest version graph) if accessible.

delete.description:
Hard-delete a workflow and its internal structure (FRO-wkf-19).

Pre-conditions (FRO-wkf-18 + FRO-arch-01 invariant):

1. Workflow must already be in ``archived`` state (force the 2-step
   pattern: archive first, then delete). Otherwise: 409
   ``not_archived``.
2. No run of this workflow may be ``qa_tracked = True`` — those runs
   and their cited artefacts are qa-locked. Otherwise: 409
   ``qa_tracked_runs_exist``.

On success: deletes the non-qa-tracked runs explicitly (FK
workflow_runs.workflow_version_id is RESTRICT to protect the audit
trail), then deletes the workflow. The CASCADE FKs on
workflow_versions / workflow_nodes / workflow_edges propagate
automatically (FRO-wkf-19 phase 1.B migration).

post.description:
Soft-archive a workflow (FRO-wkf-18).

Idempotent: archiving an already-archived workflow is a no-op (the
original ``archived_at`` timestamp is preserved). Reversible via
POST /workflows/{slug}/unarchive.

An archived workflow:
  - disappears from the default list (callers must pass
    ``include_archived=true`` to GET /workflows to see it),
  - blocks new versions (POST /workflows/{slug}/versions → 409),
  - blocks new runs (POST /workflows/{slug}/runs → 409),
  - unlocks the hard-delete path (DELETE /workflows/{slug}).

Existing versions and historical runs remain readable in any case.

Re-activate an archived workflow (FRO-wkf-18).

Idempotent: unarchiving an active workflow is a no-op. Resets
``archived_at`` to NULL — the workflow becomes editable again
(new versions, new runs allowed).

Persist a new version of the workflow graph.

Runs full static validation (typing + cycle + dangling refs). On any
violation returns 409 with a structured report.

FRO-wkf-18: refuses to save a new version on an archived workflow
(409 ``workflow_archived``). Unarchive first.

Dry-run validation of a workflow graph without persisting anything.

Runs the same static analysis as :func:`save_workflow_version` (typing
via sous-typage, cycles, dangling FK/ports, duplicate target ports,
missing io_spec) and returns the structured report as the 200 body.

The frontend "Validate" button (FRO-wkf-03) hits this endpoint so the
author can inspect issues before committing. Owner-only: only the
caller who owns the workflow can run validation on its graph.

schema.title: Response Validate Workflow Version Workflows  Slug  Validate Post

post.description:
Create a workflow run and freeze its execution plan.

Validates the submission (including the ``inputs`` dict keyed by
InputNode ``node_key``), builds the execution plan (fusion + topological
waves), stores it on the ``workflow_runs`` row, and marks the run as
``pending``. The execution loop runs separately via
``POST /workflow-runs/{id}/tick`` (Phase 4b).

Returns 201 with the created run; the caller polls ``GET /workflow-runs/{id}``
for status updates.

get.description: List workflow runs owned by the current user.

Return the detail of a run if the caller is allowed to see it.

A run is visible only to the user who submitted it (v1). Future iterations
may expose public runs of public workflows.
Expose the frozen execution plan of a run (audit / debugging).

post.description:
Advance a workflow run by one orchestration tick (Phase 4b).

Drives the run's state machine without any background task: polls
in-flight jobs, writes outputs that arrived since the last call, submits
newly ready nodes, and finalises the run's status when a terminal
condition is reached. Callers (frontend poll, cron, tests) invoke this
repeatedly until ``status`` becomes ``completed``, ``failed`` or
``cancelled``.

Access control: only the run's owner can tick (same rule as GET).
Idempotent: calling twice with no worker progress in between yields the
same state.

Mark a workflow run as part of the user's quality dossier (FRO-jobs-10).

Access control: only the run's owner can toggle qa-tracking. Idempotent:
calling twice on an already-tracked run is a no-op (returns 200 with the
current state, ``qa_tracked_at`` is left untouched to preserve the
*first toggle* audit timestamp).

Setting ``qa_tracked = True`` makes the run a member of the user's QA
dossier (FRO-arch-01) — the run is excluded from the automatic purge
(FRO-arch-02 / FRO-jobs-09) and the artefacts it cites become
qa-locked (cannot be hard-deleted while the run remains tracked).

Remove a workflow run from the user's quality dossier (FRO-jobs-10).

Access control: only the run's owner can toggle qa-tracking. Idempotent:
calling on an already-untracked run is a no-op (returns 200).

Setting ``qa_tracked = False`` releases the qa-lock on cited artefacts
(provided no other qa-tracked run still cites them). The audit fields
``qa_tracked_at`` and ``qa_tracked_by`` are reset to ``None`` — a
subsequent qa-track will record a fresh "first toggle" timestamp.

Future: the spec FRO-jobs-10 mandates a 409 when the run is attached
to an active Study (FRO-study-*). Branche dormante — Study not yet
implemented (cf. FRO-study-01 ``[future]``).

get.description:
Comprehensive health check including database connectivity.

Returns
-------
    dict: Health status of the application and its components

FunctionExecuteRequest.description: Request model for executing a function.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Map of output port name to its typed descriptor.

FunctionIoSpec.title: FunctionIoSpec
FunctionIoSpec.description:
Typed I/O declaration stored on FunctionVersion.io_spec (FRO-type-02).

Named explicitly so the generated OpenAPI schema exposes it as a reusable
reference (avoids inlining as ``dict[str, unknown]`` in the frontend
client, which would force consumers to lose the port shape).

handler.title: Handler
handler.description: Import path module:callable (mode A only ; null for modes B/C)

entrypoint.title: Entrypoint
entrypoint.description: Container CMD args for runtime kinds dockerfile/image (modes B/C)

dockerfile.title: Dockerfile
dockerfile.description: Relative path to Dockerfile (mode B only)

image_ref.title: Image Ref
image_ref.description: Full registry image reference (mode C only, e.g. 'code-aster:15.4')

io_spec.description: Typed I/O declaration (FRO-type-02). Canonical catalog types per port.

FunctionVersionDetailResponse.title: FunctionVersionDetailResponse
FunctionVersionDetailResponse.description:
Detailed view of a FunctionVersion (FRO-fn-04).

Extends ``FunctionVersionInfo`` with the parent package context and the
runtime/resource hints needed to render the function detail page without
requiring a second round-trip to fetch the package.

handler.title: Handler
handler.description: Import path module:callable (mode A only ; null for modes B/C)

entrypoint.title: Entrypoint
entrypoint.description: Container CMD args for runtime kinds dockerfile/image (modes B/C)

dockerfile.title: Dockerfile
dockerfile.description: Relative path to Dockerfile (mode B only)

image_ref.title: Image Ref
image_ref.description: Full registry image reference (mode C only, e.g. 'code-aster:15.4')

io_spec.description: Typed I/O declaration (FRO-type-02). Canonical catalog types per port.

FunctionVersionInfo.title: FunctionVersionInfo
FunctionVersionInfo.description: Information about a function version within a package.

type_expr.title: Type Expr
type_expr.description: Type expression — canonical name, parameterised (list[T]/dict[str,T]) or inline struct (mapping of field name to nested type_expr).

InputNodeSpec.title: InputNodeSpec
InputNodeSpec.description:
Payload describing the typed value produced by an InputNode.

The ``type_expr`` field accepts the same syntax as ``mecapy.yml`` typed
inputs (FRO-type-02): a plain canonical name (``"Length"``, ``"Force"``,
``"list[Force]"``) or an inline anonymous struct mapping
(``{"d": "Length", "p": "Length"}``). At validation time the expression
is parsed via :func:`types_catalog.parse_type`; the resulting
``TypeSpec`` is used for connection-compatibility checks with downstream
function-port types.

The InputNode emits its value on a single conventional output port named
:data:`INPUT_NODE_OUTPUT_PORT` (``"value"``).

JobListResponse.title: JobListResponse
JobListResponse.description: Response model for listing user jobs.

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Which stream this chunk came from.

chunk.title: Chunk
chunk.description: Raw text content as emitted by the container.

JobLogChunk.title: JobLogChunk
JobLogChunk.description:
A single stdout/stderr chunk captured during job execution.

Each chunk reflects exactly what the container emitted on that stream,
not a line — Docker may deliver partial lines or multiple lines in a
single chunk depending on output cadence.

entries.title: Entries
entries.description: Log chunks emitted since the requested index.

next_index.title: Next Index
next_index.description: Pass this value as ``since`` in the next poll to fetch only new chunks.

done.title: Done
done.description: True once the job reached a terminal status (completed / failed / timeout). Frontend should stop polling when this is True.

JobLogsResponse.title: JobLogsResponse
JobLogsResponse.description:
Incremental log payload for :spec:`FRO-jobs-08` v2 streaming.

The frontend polls ``GET /jobs/{id}/logs?since={next_index}`` every
couple of seconds while the job is running, appending ``entries`` to
its local buffer and updating ``next_index`` from the response.
Polling stops once ``done`` is True (status terminal).

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Inputs submitted at job creation (FRO-jobs-08 traceability).

JobResultResponse.title: JobResultResponse
JobResultResponse.description:
Response model for job result retrieval.

Notes
-----
Execution logs (stdout/stderr) are no longer part of this payload.
They stream live via ``GET /jobs/{id}/logs`` (:spec:`FRO-jobs-08` v2),
which is the single source of truth for log display during and after
a run. The frontend fetches both endpoints — ``/result`` for outputs
+ inputs, ``/logs`` for stdout/stderr chunks.

PackageUpdateFromGitRequest.title: PackageUpdateFromGitRequest
PackageUpdateFromGitRequest.description:
Request model for updating an existing package from a Git repository.

Unlike :class:`PackageFromGitRequest`, this does not create a new package —
it adds a new :class:`PackageVersion` to an existing package. The manifest
must reference the same package name and declare a version strictly greater
than the current one (SemVer).

PackageVisibility.title: PackageVisibility
PackageVisibility.description:
Package visibility — controls read/execute access.

- ``private`` : only the owner can view and execute.
- ``internal`` : all members of the owner's organization can view and execute.
  Requires the package to be attached to an organization.
- ``public`` : any user (including unauthenticated ones, for read) can view.
  Execution still requires authentication.

PackageVisibilityUpdate.title: PackageVisibilityUpdate
PackageVisibilityUpdate.description: Request model to update package visibility.

type.title: Type
type.description: Canonical type expression — nominal name (``Force``, ``Length``), generic (``list[Force]``, ``Literal["a", "b"]``), or a struct object.

required.title: Required
required.description: Whether the port must be provided at execution time.

TypedPortInfo.title: TypedPortInfo
TypedPortInfo.description: Single typed I/O port on a function (FRO-type-02).

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: Ordered list of categories present in the catalog.

UserInfo.title: UserInfo
UserInfo.description: User information model extracted from JWT token.

organization_slug.title: Organization Slug
organization_slug.description: Attach the workflow to an organization (optional)

WorkflowEdgeModel.title: WorkflowEdgeModel
WorkflowEdgeModel.description:
Edge connecting two node ports inside a workflow version.

``alias`` is optional and only meaningful when the target port is
dict-like (``Object`` or ``dict[str, T]``) — it overrides the dict key
used by the orchestrator when aggregating multi-fanin edges into a
single value at runtime (FRO-wkf-04c). For scalar (single-fanin) targets
the alias has no runtime effect; the editor hides the field accordingly.

WorkflowNodeModel.title: WorkflowNodeModel
WorkflowNodeModel.description:
Node inside a workflow version.

Discriminated by :attr:`node_kind`. Function-kind nodes carry
``function_version_id`` (the pinned FV whose handler executes at runtime).
Input-kind nodes carry :attr:`input_spec` (the typed value supplied by
the user at run submission). Exactly one of the two must be populated —
enforced by the post-init validator below.

WorkflowResponse.title: WorkflowResponse
WorkflowResponse.description: Full workflow detail exposed by GET /workflows/{slug}.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Values supplied to InputNodes keyed by ``node_key``. Each value is the whole typed payload (scalar / list / dict / struct) declared by the InputNode's ``input_spec.type_expr``.

WorkflowVersionRequest.title: WorkflowVersionRequest
WorkflowVersionRequest.description: Payload for saving a new workflow version (POST /workflows/{slug}/versions).

WorkflowVersionResponse.title: WorkflowVersionResponse
WorkflowVersionResponse.description: Public read shape of a workflow version including its graph.
